name: Climate Almanac
description: An open, versioned index of public climate data.

entries.title: Berkeley Earth Surface Temperature
entries.description: Independent global land and ocean surface temperature analysis with gridded fields and uncertainty estimates. Often used as a cross-check against government temperature indices.
entries.license: CC-BY-NC-SA-4.0 (see Berkeley Earth terms for commercial use)

entries.title: ERA5 Reanalysis (single levels)
entries.description: Fifth-generation ECMWF global atmospheric reanalysis at ~31 km resolution, hourly from 1940 to near-present. The standard open reanalysis for weather and climate research when a consistent gridded product is needed.

access.auth_note: Free Copernicus Climate Data Store account required
access.rate_limit: CDS queue-based retrieval; see CDS fair-use policy

entries.license: Copernicus Licence (free for research and commercial use with attribution)
entries.attribution: Copernicus Climate Change Service (C3S) (2025): ERA5 reanalysis. ECMWF.
entries.notes: Native hourly fields; catalogue also offers monthly aggregates.

entries.title: CPC Long-Lead Climate Outlooks
entries.description: NOAA Climate Prediction Center seasonal and long-lead outlooks for temperature, precipitation, and drought across the United States. Operational guidance products for planners and researchers tracking climate variability.
entries.notes: Parent /products/predictions/ path returned HTTP 403 to automated probes but this long-range subdirectory responded 200 on 2026-06-29.

entries.title: HadCRUT5 Global Temperature Dataset
entries.description: Met Office Hadley Centre / UEA CRU gridded near-surface temperature anomalies on a 5° grid. One of the long-running global temperature records used in IPCC assessments alongside NASA and NOAA products.
entries.publisher: UK Met Office Hadley Centre / Climatic Research Unit
entries.license: Open Government Licence v3.0 (UK Met Office data)
entries.attribution: HadCRUT5 — Met Office Hadley Centre / University of East Anglia

entries.title: GISS Surface Temperature Analysis (GISTEMP v4)
entries.description: NASA's global land–ocean surface temperature anomaly product, widely cited in annual global temperature reporting. Gridded anomalies and station-based analysis with open data files and documented methods.
entries.publisher: NASA Goddard Institute for Space Studies (GISS)
entries.attribution: NASA GISS Surface Temperature Analysis (GISTEMP Team, 2025)

entries.title: GRACE/GRACE-FO Land Mass Concentration (Mascon) Time Series
entries.description: NASA/JPL gridded time series of terrestrial water storage changes from the GRACE and GRACE-FO gravity missions. Used for drought, groundwater, and ice mass balance studies at regional to global scales.
entries.publisher: NASA JPL Physical Oceanography Distributed Active Archive Center (PO.DAAC)

access.auth_note: Free NASA Earthdata Login required for PO.DAAC downloads

entries.attribution: NASA JPL GRACE/GRACE-FO Mascon solutions via PO.DAAC
entries.notes: Legacy grace.jpl.nasa.gov product pages returned 404 on 2026-06-29; PO.DAAC is the current canonical distribution path for this mascon release.

entries.title: NOAA Climate Data Online (CDO)
entries.description: Searchable archive and API for NOAA/NCEI station, radar, and derived climate datasets. The general-purpose access path for many NCEI holdings beyond single-product landing pages.

access.auth_note: Free NCEI token required for API access
access.rate_limit: 5 requests per second; 10,000 requests per day (documented CDO limits)

entries.attribution: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information, Climate Data Online

entries.title: Global Ocean Heat Content (NCEI)
entries.description: Observed ocean heat content anomalies for the upper ocean layers, a key indicator of Earth's energy imbalance and climate change. Maintained by NCEI as part of global ocean monitoring products.
entries.attribution: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information, Global Ocean Heat Content

entries.title: NOAA Storm Events Database
entries.description: Official record of severe storms and significant weather phenomena across the United States, with fatalities, injuries, and property damage. Widely used for hazard climatology and disaster research.
entries.attribution: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information, Storm Events Database

entries.title: Billion-Dollar Weather and Climate Disasters
entries.description: Database of US weather/climate disasters each exceeding $1B in damages (CPI-adjusted), 1980-present, with event-level cost, deaths, and type. The canonical record for US climate-disaster cost trends.
entries.attribution: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information (NCEI), U.S. Billion-Dollar Weather and Climate Disasters
entries.notes: THE THESIS ENTRY. NOAA announced in 2025 it would stop updating this database. Historical data through 2024 remains reachable but is no longer maintained — hence status=frozen. This is exactly the kind of small, high-value, at-risk artifact that should be prioritized for an actual mirror (it is a single modest CSV). Verify current reachability before relying on it; if the page 404s, flip status to dark and populate archive.wayback_url + archive.mirror.

entries.title: Climate at a Glance — Global & National Time Series
entries.description: Monthly temperature and precipitation anomalies for the globe, nations, US regions, states, and counties. NCEI's flagship public-facing trend product; the source behind most "warmest year on record" reporting.

access.rate_limit: NCEI access API, no published hard limit; be polite

entries.attribution: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information, Climate at a Glance
entries.notes: Core dataset — high priority to keep mapped. Time series are small (CSV per region) and would be cheap to mirror if it ever darkens.

entries.title: Global Historical Climatology Network — Daily (GHCN-Daily)
entries.description: Daily station observations of temperature, precipitation, and snow for tens of thousands of land stations worldwide. The backbone of many global and regional climate analyses and a primary input to gridded products.
entries.attribution: NOAA National Centers for Environmental Information, GHCN-Daily
entries.notes: Bulk files are served under /data/global-historical-climatology-network-daily/. Station metadata and documentation ship with the download bundles.

entries.title: Global Monitoring Laboratory — CO₂ Trends
entries.description: Atmospheric carbon dioxide measurements from the Mauna Loa and global in situ network, including the iconic Keeling Curve. The reference time series for anthropogenic CO₂ increase in public communication and research.

coverage.spatial: global (in situ sites; Mauna Loa flagship)

entries.attribution: NOAA Global Monitoring Laboratory, Carbon Cycle Greenhouse Gases

entries.title: North American Snow Cover Extent (G02135)
entries.description: Weekly snow cover extent for North America from the Rutgers Global Snow Lab product archived at NSIDC. Complements sea-ice products for cryosphere and hydrology monitoring.
entries.attribution: Rutgers Global Snow Lab / NSIDC G02135

entries.title: Sea Ice Index
entries.description: Daily and monthly Arctic and Antarctic sea ice extent and concentration, with anomaly fields and trend imagery. The standard reference for sea-ice decline; widely cited and surfaced by climate.gov.
entries.attribution: Fetterer, F. et al. Sea Ice Index, NSIDC, Boulder, Colorado USA
entries.notes: Hosted at NSIDC (a university-based DAAC), so somewhat insulated from federal site shutdowns — but funded partly through NOAA/NASA, so not immune. Worth watching.

entries.title: U.S. Drought Monitor
entries.description: Weekly map and tabular summary of drought conditions across the United States, blending physical indicators and expert synthesis. The operational drought status record cited in media, agriculture, and policy.
entries.license: public-domain (US Gov partner product; see site terms)
entries.attribution: U.S. Drought Monitor — USDA, NOAA, and University of Nebraska–Lincoln

entries.title: USGS National Water Information System (NWIS)
entries.description: Real-time and historical streamflow, groundwater, water quality, and related measurements from USGS monitoring networks. The primary federal portal for US hydrologic observations used in drought, flood, and climate-impact studies.

access.rate_limit: Documented NWIS web service limits; be polite

entries.attribution: U.S. Geological Survey National Water Information System
